title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. maximum
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. maximum, also known as the maximum oxeye daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is commonly found in grasslands and open areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. maximum include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. maximum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
